description: Bacterial spot is an important disease of tomato that can affect leaves, stems, and fruits. Warm, wet weather favors disease development and spread.
symptoms: Small dark or water-soaked spots appear on leaves
Leaf spots may become brown or black and can be surrounded by yellow tissue
Severe infection can cause leaf yellowing and defoliation
Small raised or scabby lesions may develop on fruits
Fruit lesions can become rough and corky
Stem and petiole lesions may occur

favorable_conditions.rainfall: Frequent rain and splashing water promote spread
favorable_conditions.additional_conditions: Warm temperatures combined with wet foliage create high-risk conditions

spread: Contaminated seed and transplants can introduce the pathogen
Rain splash spreads bacteria between plants
Overhead irrigation can contribute to spread
Workers and equipment can mechanically spread bacteria when plants are wet
prevention: Use pathogen-free seed and healthy transplants
Rotate away from tomato and pepper where appropriate
Avoid handling plants when foliage is wet
Sanitize tools and equipment where practical

immediate_actions: Remove severely infected plants in small plantings where practical
cultural_control: Use drip or soil-level irrigation where practical
biological_control: Use locally registered biological bactericides where available

chemical_control.recommended: Copper-based bactericides or other locally registered products may help suppress bacterial spot but may not eliminate established infections.
chemical_control.warning: Copper resistance can occur in Xanthomonas populations. Follow local agricultural recommendations and product labels and avoid unnecessary repeated applications.

severity_guidance.low: A few localized spots. Monitor closely and reduce conditions that promote splash dispersal.
severity_guidance.moderate: Multiple leaves or plants affected. Strengthen sanitation and locally recommended management.
severity_guidance.high: Widespread foliage or fruit infection. Seek agricultural guidance and implement integrated bacterial disease management.

farmer_advice: Prevention is especially important because bacterial diseases are difficult to cure after infection. Start with clean seed or transplants and minimize water splash.
disclaimer: AI identification is not definitive. Bacterial spot can resemble other tomato leaf diseases, including early blight, Septoria leaf spot, and target spot.
